streams.description:
An array of connection element routes that specify the run path of the connection.
The streams collection is the heart of a connection, it puts the pieces together to create the data path.

There are 4 possible stream types. There can only be one of each, and they must be defined in the following order:
 - Source (required)
 - Filter (optional, and only stream part where you can have more than one.)
 - Format (optional for some destinations)
 - Destination (required)

Failure to follow this pattern will result in failure.

post.description: Bulk Action - Perform a bulk update / action on a group of connections by providing one of the specified actions and an array of connection ids. The available actions that may be provided to the API include:    Run - Starts execution of the connections.   Stop - Stops execution of connections if currently running.   Enable - Enables the connections.   Disable - Disables the connections.   Delete - Deletes the connections.   Take Ownership - Takes ownership of the connections.

batchSize.description: The number of records to send in each HTTP request. Define either `batchSize` or `batchSizeBytes`, but not both. Defining `batchSize` creates multiple `POST` requests. Each `POST` request contains the `batchSize` number of records until all records are sent. If `batchSize` is `undefined` or `0`, all records are sent in one `POST` request.

batchSizeBytes.description: The target size of an HTTP request body in bytes. Define either `batchSize` or `batchSizeBytes`, but not both. Defining `batchSizeBytes` creates multiple `POST` requests. Each `POST` request contains up to the `batchSizeBytes` number of bytes until all records are sent. A POST request can exceed this number if a single record is larger than the `batchSizeBytes` number of bytes. If `batchSizeBytes` is `undefined` or `0`, all records are sent in one `POST` request.
